The uncomfortable truth

Most people will never lose weight walking.
And it's not their fault.

The 10,000 step goal was invented in 1965 by a pedometer company trying to sell more devices. It was never based on science. Never tested. Never proven. Just a round number that sounded impressive enough to stick.

For over 60 years, millions of people have been chasing it. Feeling guilty when they fall short. Giving up when life gets in the way. Wondering why the weight isn't shifting despite all those steps.

"Walking slow for a long time barely touches your fat stores. Your body adapts, your metabolism flatlines, and you end up burning less than you think for more effort than you want to give."

Here's the thing nobody tells you: the problem was never your effort. It was always the method.

Real app. Real screens. This is exactly what you get. ↓ Scroll to see it in action.

The Method That Changes Everything

Dr. Nose spent 20 years finding the answer.
We put it in your pocket.

Dr. Hiroshi Nose at Japan's Shinshu University discovered something counterintuitive and devastatingly effective: alternating fast and slow walking at exact intervals forces your body into a fat-burning state it simply cannot reach through steady plodding.

His participants burned more fat, dropped blood pressure, lowered cholesterol, built real leg strength — and formed habits that actually lasted. All in 30 minutes a day. No gym. No equipment.

"Thousands of steps never changed your body.
Thirty minutes of rhythm will."

Dead simple. That's the point.

3 minutes fast.
3 minutes slow.
5 times. Done.

No decisions to make. No steps to count. No guilt if you don't hit a number. Just start and follow the rhythm — Stride Kai cues every transition with audio and vibration so your phone never leaves your pocket.

1

Push — Fast Walk 3 min

Walk as fast as you can sustain. Your heart rate climbs. Your body is forced out of its comfort zone.

2

Recover — Slow Walk 3 min

Drop the pace completely. This is where fat oxidation peaks. Most people skip this phase. That's why they fail.

×5

Repeat — 5 Cycles = 30 min

5 cycles. 30 minutes. Then you're done. Stride Kai cues every transition. Phone stays in your pocket the whole time.

The Research Behind It

20 years of science. One app.

Dr. Hiroshi Nose published his interval walking research after two decades of clinical study at Shinshu University in Japan. The results were replicated, peer-reviewed, and clear: alternating fast and slow walking outperforms steady-pace walking on every meaningful health marker.

Stride Kai implements his exact protocol. Same timing. Same structure. Same results — without a lab coat or a treadmill.

"
The 10,000 step goal was invented in 1965 by a pedometer company trying to sell more devices. It was never based on science. Sixty years later, millions of people are still paying the price.
Stride Kai exists to fix that.
